title: Privacy Policy

seo.metaDescription: How ImperialPedia collects, uses, and protects your data. Covers GDPR and CCPA rights, cookies, analytics, advertising, third-party services, and your choices.
seo.searchIntent: Informational/Legal — readers and regulators want to understand data practices.

excerpt: This Privacy Policy explains what data ImperialPedia collects, how we use it, the third-party services involved, and the rights you have under laws such as the GDPR and CCPA.
intro:
Your privacy matters to us. This Privacy Policy explains what information ImperialPedia ("ImperialPedia," "we," "us," or "our") collects when you visit ImperialPedia.com, how we use it, who we share it with, and the choices and rights you have over your data.

By using our website, you agree to the practices described here. If you do not agree, please discontinue use of the site. This policy may be updated from time to time, and the "last updated" note will reflect the most recent revision.

This document is provided for transparency and general information. It is a template intended to be reviewed and adapted to your specific operations and jurisdictions with qualified legal counsel.

sections.heading: Information We Collect
paragraphs: We collect information in a few ways. Most of it is gathered automatically as you browse; some is provided directly by you.

subsections.heading: Information you provide
paragraphs: When you contact us, subscribe to updates, or submit a form, you may share details such as your name, email address, and the contents of your message. You choose what to share.

subsections.heading: Information collected automatically
paragraphs: Like most websites, we automatically receive technical data such as your IP address, browser type, device information, pages visited, referring URLs, and timestamps. This is collected through server logs, cookies, and similar technologies.

sections.heading: How We Use Your Information
paragraphs: We use the information we collect to operate, maintain, and improve ImperialPedia; to respond to your inquiries; to understand how our content is used so we can make it better; to ensure security and prevent abuse; and to comply with legal obligations.

Where required by law, we rely on a lawful basis for processing — typically your consent (for example, for non-essential cookies and marketing), our legitimate interests in running and improving the site, or compliance with a legal obligation.

sections.heading: Cookies and Tracking Technologies
paragraphs: Cookies are small text files stored on your device. We use essential cookies that make the site function, and, with your consent where required, non-essential cookies for analytics and advertising.

You can control cookies through your browser settings and, where offered, through our cookie consent tool. Blocking some cookies may affect how parts of the site work. For more detail on the specific cookies in use, refer to any cookie banner or settings panel presented on the site.

callout.title: Your choice
callout.text: Where consent is required, non-essential cookies are only set after you opt in, and you can withdraw consent at any time through your browser or our cookie controls.

sections.heading: Analytics
paragraphs: We may use analytics services (for example, privacy-conscious web analytics or providers such as Google Analytics) to understand aggregate traffic patterns — which articles are popular, how readers navigate, and where we can improve.

These services may set their own cookies and process data such as your IP address and on-site behavior. We use this information in aggregate to improve our content and experience, not to identify you personally.

sections.heading: Advertising
paragraphs: If we display advertising, ad partners may use cookies or similar technologies to show relevant ads and measure their performance. Depending on your location and consent, this may involve personalized advertising.

You can typically opt out of personalized advertising through industry tools such as the Digital Advertising Alliance (optout.aboutads.info), the Network Advertising Initiative, or your device and browser settings. Where required, personalized advertising will only occur with your consent.

sections.heading: Third-Party Services
paragraphs: We rely on trusted third-party providers to deliver and improve the site — for example, hosting and content delivery, analytics, email/communication tools, and, where applicable, advertising networks. These providers process data on our behalf or as independent controllers under their own policies.
We encourage you to review the privacy policies of any third-party services you interact with. We are not responsible for the privacy practices of external sites we link to.

sections.heading: How We Share Information
paragraphs: We do not sell your personal information in the traditional sense. We share data only as needed: with service providers who help us run the site, when required by law or to protect our rights, and in connection with a business transfer (such as a merger or acquisition).
Note that under some laws, such as the CCPA/CPRA, certain ad-related data sharing may be classified as a 'sale' or 'sharing.' Where that applies, we honor opt-out rights as described below.

sections.heading: Your Rights Under the GDPR
paragraphs: If you are in the European Economic Area or the United Kingdom, you have rights over your personal data, including the right to access, correct, delete, restrict, or object to processing; the right to data portability; and the right to withdraw consent at any time.

You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority. To exercise any of these rights, contact us using the details below, and we will respond in line with applicable law.

sections.heading: Your Rights Under the CCPA/CPRA
paragraphs: If you are a California resident, you have the right to know what personal information we collect and how it is used, the right to request deletion, the right to correct inaccurate information, and the right to opt out of the 'sale' or 'sharing' of personal information, as those terms are defined under California law.
We will not discriminate against you for exercising these rights. To make a request, contact us using the details below; we may need to verify your identity before fulfilling it.

sections.heading: Data Retention and Security
paragraphs: We keep personal information only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this policy or as required by law, after which it is deleted or anonymized.
We use reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect your data. However, no method of transmission or storage is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

sections.heading: Children's Privacy
paragraphs: ImperialPedia is intended for a general, adult audience and is not directed at children under the age of 13 (or the equivalent minimum age in your jurisdiction). We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If you believe a child has provided us with personal data, please contact us so we can remove it.

sections.heading: International Data Transfers
paragraphs: We may process and store information in countries other than the one in which you reside. Where we transfer personal data across borders, we take steps to ensure it receives an adequate level of protection consistent with applicable law, such as standard contractual clauses where appropriate.

sections.heading: Changes to This Policy
paragraphs: We may update this Privacy Policy to reflect changes in our practices, technology, or legal requirements. When we do, we will revise the 'last updated' date and, where appropriate, provide a more prominent notice. Your continued use of the site after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

sections.heading: Contact Us
paragraphs: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or wish to exercise your privacy rights, please reach us through our [contact page](/contact). We take privacy inquiries seriously and will respond as promptly as we can.

faq.question: Does ImperialPedia sell my personal data?
faq.answer: We do not sell personal information in the traditional sense. Some advertising-related data sharing may qualify as a 'sale' or 'sharing' under laws like California's CCPA/CPRA; where that applies, we honor your right to opt out.

faq.question: How do I opt out of cookies or tracking?
faq.answer: You can manage cookies through your browser settings and any cookie consent tool shown on the site. Where consent is required, non-essential cookies are only set after you opt in, and you can withdraw consent at any time.

faq.question: What rights do I have over my data?
faq.answer: Depending on where you live, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, restrict, or port your data, and to opt out of certain sharing. EEA/UK residents have GDPR rights; California residents have CCPA/CPRA rights. Contact us to exercise them.

faq.question: How can I contact ImperialPedia about privacy?
faq.answer: Use our contact page to submit privacy questions or rights requests. We may need to verify your identity before acting on certain requests, in line with applicable law.

conclusion: Transparency is part of how ImperialPedia earns your trust. This policy reflects our commitment to handling your data responsibly and giving you meaningful control over it. If anything here is unclear, please reach out through our [contact page](/contact) — and when you're ready, return to our [Personal Finance](/personal-finance) guides.

externalSources.name: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) — California Attorney General
